title: heptapleurum siamense
intro: Heptapleurum siamense, also known as the Siamese maidenhair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Heptapleurum siamense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
